causeDescription.hacking:
Breach caused by external attackers.
ex) SQL injection, ransomware, phishing, etc.
causeDescription.insider:
Intentional data theft by employees or contractors.
ex) USB exfiltration, privilege abuse, etc.
causeDescription.negligence:
Breach due to poor security management.
ex) Failure to revoke access, misconfigured settings, etc.
causeDescription.technical:
System or service technical vulnerabilities.
ex) Missing API auth, library exploits, etc.
causeDescription.unknown: Cause unknown or investigation still ongoing.

search.placeholderDesktop: Enter company name, service name, etc.

home.bannerHeadline2:
Uncovering why breaches keep happening—
and what it takes to stay protected.
home.bannerDesc:
Real incidents. Real data. Learn what's behind
today's biggest security threats and how to defend against them.
home.listDesc: Browse real security incidents that occurred in South Korea and explore their scale and root causes.
home.statisticsDesc: Understand the major trends and root causes of security threats through type-specific incident statistics.
home.serviceTitle: Useful services to protect your data
home.serviceDesc: Check and respond through official channels.

register.title: Submit a Case
register.subtitle: Share a security incident not yet added to SecurityLog.

services.title: Find My Leaked Info
services.description: A service to check whether your personal information has been leaked

services.title: Privacy Violation Report Center
services.description: A channel to report and consult on privacy violations such as leaks and misuse

services.title: Personal Information Protection Commission
services.description: A central government body overseeing privacy policy, law enforcement, and system improvements
